issues.comment: The method `include_website_name` is missing the `?` suffix required by ActiveModel::Serializer (0.8.x) for conditional include predicates. Without the trailing `?`, the framework never calls this method, making it dead code. It happens to work by coincidence because the parent class `UserCardSerializer` already defines `include_website_name?` (with `?`) via its `untrusted_attributes` macro. The developer's intent (`website.present?`) differs from what actually runs (`website_name.present?` with a trust-level check). If someone later removes `website_name` from `untrusted_attributes` in the parent, this method would silently stop working as a conditional include. Also, if someone adds `?` to "fix" the name, it would override the parent's trust-level check, creating a security regression (TL0 users' website would be exposed to anonymous visitors).
